stemHtml:
Refer to figure or VFR chart ED-4 from the Jeppesen GSPRM 2017.
A pilot is flying a leg between waypoints A (N48°53' E012°59') and B (N48°26' E014°08') at 1000 ft AGL. The planned flight time was calculated to be 26 minutes and 30 seconds. During the flight, the pilot notices that they arrive at waypoint B earlier than planned. What is the most likely reason for this?

Planning data:
W/V at FL100: 070°/30 kt
GS: 120 kt

options.a: The W/V for planning was taken from FL100, with less headwind in flight at 1000 ft AGL.
options.b: The actual wind is veering during the flight.
options.c: The actual wind direction is the same as the true track angle.
options.d: The wind speed during the flight at 1000 ft AGL is higher than the wind speed of 30 kt taken for the planning.
